title: rosa × dumetorum var. gracilenta
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. gracilenta, also known as the dwarf wild rose, is a variant of the Rosa × dumetorum plant, a hybrid of Rosa glauca and Rosa rubiginosa. It is a shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anthocyanins and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Rosa × dumetorum var. gracilenta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
